hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by enhancing hydrophobic complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 pockets, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target interactions by avoiding polar contacts with SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding can be achieved while reducing off-target interactions. However, structural quality must be improved to increase pass rates and ensure that high ddG candidates are physically viable.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures are likely due to poor geometry or clashes in the designed peptides. Lowering the clash threshold will enforce better structural quality and reduce false positives.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing design on these positions will allow us to introduce mutations that reduce off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
